generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance SteeringServoController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Manages direct interfacing with the vehicle's steering mechanism for real-time positional adjustments via ROS2-based control loops"
]

instance MotorActuatorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Precise speed regulation via Vedder Electronic Speed Controller, converting desired speed commands into motor voltage commands"
]

instance ActuationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Converts steering/motor commands into physical actuation signals for the vehicle chassis, interfacing with ROS2 safety middleware for fail-safe mechanisms"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor integrated into autonomy system for 2D plane representation of point coordinates, operating within 100ms latency constraint"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera providing 3D visualization data for perception tasks, synchronized with ROS2 for real-time processing"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance MotorStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing motor actuation signals and vehicle state metrics (speed, position) for ACC control feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationData
]

instance SteeringStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor interfacing with steering servo inputs for real-time positional adjustments and control feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringActuationData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR
    base:desc "Pre-processed LiDAR point clouds enabling real-time object detection and tracking via clustering algorithms"
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthCamera
    base:desc "Depth camera data streams for 3D visualization and perception processing"
]

instance MotorActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStateSensor
    base:desc "Raw motor actuation signals and vehicle state metrics (speed, position) for ACC control"
]

instance SteeringActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringStateSensor
    base:desc "Steering servo input data for real-time positional adjustments"
]

instance ProcessedLiDARClusters : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR
    base:desc "Clustering and tracking results of LiDAR point clouds for object detection in virtual cockpit"
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationData, SteeringActuationData
    base:desc "Feedback signals from motor/steering actuators for closed-loop control"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloudModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model representing 2D plane representations of point coordinates from LiDAR sensors"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingFE
]

instance DepthCameraModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D visualization model for depth camera data integration"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model capturing vehicle state metrics (speed, position) for monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MonitoringDashboard
]

instance ObjectDetectionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model implementing clustering and tracking algorithms for LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringFE
]

instance ACCControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model for adaptive cruise control decision-making"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFE
]

instance RealTimeTrajectoryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model tracking vehicle state trajectories for monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MonitoringDashboard
]

instance LiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted at high resolution for object detection"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingFE
]

instance DepthData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw depth camera data streams for 3D visualization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

instance MotorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw motor actuation signals and vehicle state metrics for ACC control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorActuationData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFE
]

instance SteeringData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw steering servo input data for real-time adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringActuationData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFE
]

instance ProcessedLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR clustering and tracking results for object detection"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedLiDARClusters
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationFE
]

instance ActuationFeedback : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Feedback signals from motor/steering actuators for closed-loop control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MonitoringDashboard
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Centralized middleware for real-time data exchange, service orchestration, and control loop synchronization between physical and digital twins using ROS2 topics and action servers"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataPreProcessingFE, VisualizationFE, ActuationFE, MonitoringDashboard
]

instance NVIDIAJetson : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge processing unit for real-time AI inference (object detection, ACC algorithm execution) with low-latency requirements"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringFE, ACCControlModel
]

instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Used for historical data logging, performance analytics, and temporal correlation analysis of sensor/control data"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringDashboard
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataMonitoring : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous streaming and visualization of sensor data (LiDAR/camera outputs, vehicle state variables) via ROS2 topics and SQL databases"
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicTrafficVisualization, ObstacleDetectionDashboard, VehicleStateMetrics
]

instance ComputationOffloadingForACC : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delegation of perception-heavy tasks (path planning, obstacle classification) to edge/cloud infrastructure while maintaining real-time control"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCAlgorithmTesting, FirmwareUpdateSimulation, PerceptionTaskOffloading
]

instance RemoteManagementAndControl : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Secure teleoperation and firmware updates for physical twin via ROS2-based communication protocols"
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakingTrigger, DistributedDecisionMaking, FailSafeMechanisms
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DynamicTrafficVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of traffic density and obstacle detection in the virtual cockpit"
]

instance ObstacleDetectionDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic visualization of detected obstacles and their trajectories in real-time"
]

instance VehicleStateMetrics : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of vehicle speed, acceleration, and position metrics"
]

instance ACCAlgorithmTesting : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Simulation environment allowing firmware updates and testing of new ACC algorithms without physical vehicle intervention"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FirmwareUpdateSimulation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Virtual testing platform for firmware updates and system integration validation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerceptionTaskOffloading : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Offloading computationally intensive perception tasks to edge/cloud infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyBrakingTrigger : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic emergency braking system triggered via digital twin coordination"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DistributedDecisionMaking : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote coordination of critical ACC decisions across distributed entities"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FailSafeMechanisms : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Implicit fail-safe mechanisms for critical ACC operations via ROS2 safety middleware"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Functional Entities
instance DataPreProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Component responsible for filtering, clustering, and noise reduction of raw LiDAR data for accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, NVIDIAJetson
]

instance PointCloudClusteringFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Specialized component implementing clustering and tracking algorithms for LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, NVIDIAJetson
]

instance PresentationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Transforms processed data into 3D visualizations for the virtual cockpit"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, SQLDatabase
]

instance ActuationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Processes steering/motor commands and converts them into physical actuation signals"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, NVIDIAJetson
]

instance MonitoringDashboard : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Aggregates real-time data into dashboards for vehicle state monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, SQLDatabase
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Handles serialization and unit conversions for data transmission between entities"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance VisualizationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Handles 3D visualization components of the user interface"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

// C13: Specific Functional Entities
instance UserInterfaceFeatureEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Single User Interface component split into 3D Visualization and Control Panel subcomponents for user interaction"
    base:contains VisualizationFE
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Manages data management and control mechanisms for the digital twin system"
    base:contains DataPreProcessingFE, VisualizationFE, ActuationFE, MonitoringDashboard
]

instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Consolidates compute, sensor, and actuator resources for the physical vehicle"
    base:contains HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera, MotorActuatorController, SteeringServoController
]

// C14: Environment
instance AutonomySystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera, MotorActuatorController, SteeringServoController,
              ROS2Middleware, NVIDIAJetson, SQLDatabase, UserInterfaceFeatureEntity
]

instance ACCSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DigitalTwinEntity, PhysicalTwinEntity, DataPreProcessingFE, PointCloudClusteringFE,
              PresentationFE, ActuationFE, MonitoringDashboard, DataTranslationFE
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** enables remote management of the Autonomous Cruise Control (ACC) subsystem via the digital twin. Commands such as enabling/disabling ACC, modifying desired speed, and triggering emergency braking are sent from the user interface to the ACC Controlling FE. These control messages are validated and authenticated through the **Data Translation FE**, ensuring only authorized commands are executed. Feedback from the physical vehicle is translated back into the digital twin via the same **Data Translation FE**, confirming command execution (e.g., sending the desired speed back to the vehicle). No explicit conflict resolution mechanisms are described, but the system relies on the ACC Controlling FE to handle discrepancies between digital and physical states."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **hardware-imposed time synchronization constraints** with a **100 ms accuracy requirement** between physical and digital components. This is critical for enabling computation offloading in safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (beyond 100 ms) is mandated by industry standards. The system relies on **clock synchronization protocols** (though not explicitly named) to ensure temporal alignment across distributed hardware components, including sensors, actuators, and ROS2-based software entities. The temporal granularity is aligned with real-time processing demands, with no explicit mention of higher-frequency synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) beyond the stated 100 ms baseline due to hardware limitations."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows an **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)**-inspired design, mapping software components to **Feature Entities (FEs)** and deploying them as **ROS2 packages** across hardware components. The architecture is organized into three **entity-based structures**:
  - **User Entity**: Contains the **User Interface FE**, instantiated in **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** components.
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosts data management and control mechanisms.
  - **Physical Twin Entity**: Incorporates general automotive functionality (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) alongside device-specific resources (sensors/actuators).
  The system deviates from ISO 23247 by treating the **Physical Twin Entity** as a broader resource container rather than a strictly resource-specific domain. **Component integration** relies on ROS2’s modular node architecture, enabling inter-FE communication via standardized messaging protocols. **Resource management** is implicit through hardware deployment per entity, with no explicit orchestration layer mentioned. The design prioritizes **entity-based ownership** over cross-entity coordination, though ROS2’s distributed execution model facilitates loose coupling."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard defines a **reference architecture** (Part 2) that organizes the Digital Twin (DT) system around an **entity-based framework**, where each **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**—such as equipment, facilities, processes, or personnel—is represented by a **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)**. The standard does not explicitly outline lifecycle stages like 'Design,' 'Deployment,' or 'Operational' phases but instead emphasizes the **modular composition of entities** and their **functional entities (FEs)**. Each OME is linked to a **Data Collection and Device Control Entity (DCCE)**, enabling real-time synchronization between physical and digital representations. The lifecycle governance is implicitly governed by the **ISO 23247 framework’s modularity**, where entities are dynamically adaptable to changes in OMEs, but no explicit lifecycle management methodology (e.g., versioning, transition protocols) is specified. The standard’s focus remains on the **architectural decomposition** rather than prescriptive lifecycle phases."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard does not prescribe specific guidelines for implementing Functional Entities (FEs) or validating Digital Twin models. The system’s fidelity and validity are implicitly supported by the **public demonstration** of the Digital Twin, though no explicit validation methods (e.g., uncertainty quantification, error detection) or quality assurance processes are detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin technical connection** employs a hybrid network architecture combining **ROS2** for internal component communication within the software stack and **Wi-Fi UDP** for inter-entity communication between the digital twin and external systems. The system maintains a **network latency constraint of at most 100 ms** to ensure real-time synchronization between physical and virtual entities. Data translation and serialization are handled by the **Data Translation FE**, which aligns message types and performs unit conversions. No explicit mention of bandwidth requirements or redundancy mechanisms is provided, but the architecture prioritizes low-latency, reliable communication for critical vehicle state monitoring and control."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin hosting and deployment** utilize an **edge computing infrastructure**, specifically the **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer**, for processing and storage tasks. The deployment model supports real-time processing of sensor data (e.g., LiDAR, motor/steering inputs) with minimal latency. While cloud offloading is implied for scalability, the documents do not specify cloud providers, orchestration tools, or additional hardware details. The system ensures availability and reliability through the embedded edge deployment, with no explicit mention of redundancy mechanisms or storage capacity specifications beyond the edge hardware."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard (Part 2) is the **primary reference framework** for Digital Twin systems in manufacturing, defining an **entity-based reference architecture** for DTs. The standard does not mandate **certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** but outlines **interoperability standards** via the **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA, IEC 62541)** for data exchange between entities. While the standard permits **custom communication layers**, OPC UA is a **recommended protocol** for ensuring compatibility between DCCE and DTE. No other industry standards (e.g., ISO 9001) are referenced in the provided documents."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system must adhere to **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** performance requirements, ensuring compliance with safety-critical operational standards. This includes implementing **emergency braking commands** as a remote management function (Requirement R5), where the system must respond to safety-critical events with immediate, fail-safe actions.

For **cybersecurity and safety**, the system must respect the **safety-critical nature of the underlying automotive infrastructure**, particularly in **v2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) communication environments**, as defined in **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**. While no explicit mention of **ISO 26262** (Functional Safety for Road Vehicles) or **Automotive Safety Integrity Levels (ASIL)** is present, the context implies adherence to **ISO 23247** (safety requirements for connected vehicles) and **ISO 15622** for ACC-specific safety protocols. The system must incorporate **risk assessment approaches** to validate safety integrity, though no specific methodologies (e.g., FMEA, HAZOP) or thresholds (e.g., ASIL assignments) are detailed in the provided documents.

No explicit mention of **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., TLS, V2X encryption standards) or **access control mechanisms** (e.g., authentication frameworks like ETSI ISG V2X’s **V2X Authentication Framework**) is found in the documents. The focus remains on **safety-critical system validation** rather than broader cybersecurity governance."
]
